skill.name: mcp-builder
skill.description: Guide for building high-quality Model Context Protocol servers with TypeScript or Python, including tool design, transports, testing, and evaluations.
skill.summary: Build well-designed MCP servers for external services with practical SDK, testing, and evaluation guidance.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: This is an official Anthropic skill and is suitable for publication. Static flags relate to expected MCP builder workflows: fetching public documentation, launching user-selected test servers, and passing environment variables during evaluation.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to design MCP servers that agents can use reliably. This skill provides practical guidance for tool design, SDK setup, testing, and evaluation.
actual_capabilities: Guides MCP server planning around API coverage, workflow tools, naming, and context management.
Explains TypeScript and Python implementation patterns using the official MCP SDKs.
Covers input schemas, output schemas, structured responses, annotations, pagination, and error handling.
Recommends transport choices for local stdio servers and remote streamable HTTP servers.
Includes an evaluation workflow and helper scripts for testing whether agents can use MCP tools effectively.
Provides security guidance for authentication, input validation, DNS rebinding protection, and safe error messages.
limitations: It is a guidance skill, not a complete MCP server generator.
External API details must still be researched for each target service.
Evaluation scripts require a working MCP server and valid Claude API access.
Users must review generated server code for service-specific security and deployment needs.

use_cases.title: Create a service integration
use_cases.description: Plan and implement an MCP server that exposes a third-party API through clear, typed tools.

use_cases.title: Improve tool usability
use_cases.description: Review tool names, schemas, pagination, and error messages so agents can complete tasks with fewer failures.

use_cases.title: Validate agent performance
use_cases.description: Create read-only evaluation questions and run them against an MCP server to measure task success.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a basic MCP server
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new integration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan an MCP server for [service]. Identify core resources, common actions, authentication needs, and the first tools to build.

prompt_templates.title: Design tool schemas
prompt_templates.prompt: Design MCP tools for these [service] API endpoints: [endpoints]. Include tool names, inputs, outputs, annotations, pagination, and error behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Implement with an SDK
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced implementation in TypeScript or Python.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build the MCP server structure for [service] using [TypeScript/Python]. Include authentication, shared client utilities, schemas, tool registration, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate server quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 10 read-only evaluation questions for my MCP server. Make each question realistic, complex, independent, stable, and verifiable.

output_examples.input: Plan an MCP server for a project management API.
output: A prioritized tool inventory grouped by projects, tasks, users, and comments.
Recommended naming patterns such as service_list_tasks and service_create_comment.
Authentication, pagination, and error-handling notes for the first implementation pass.

output_examples.input: Review these proposed MCP tools for quality.
output: Specific feedback on ambiguous tool names and missing service prefixes.
Schema improvements for required fields, limits, examples, and output structure.
Risk notes for destructive operations and suggested tool annotations.

output_examples.input: Create evaluation questions for this MCP server.
output: Ten read-only questions that require multiple tool calls.
Stable expected answers that can be checked directly.
A short explanation of why each question tests real agent capability.

best_practices: Start with API research, then choose tools that balance broad coverage with high-value workflows.
Use clear service-prefixed names, schema validation, pagination, structured outputs, and actionable errors.
Test with realistic read-only evaluations before releasing an MCP server to users.
anti_patterns: Do not expose vague tools that hide their side effects or return unstructured large responses.
Do not skip authentication, input validation, pagination, or safe error handling.
Do not rely only on simple smoke tests when agents must complete complex multi-step tasks.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you design, implement, test, and evaluate MCP servers that connect AI agents to external services.

faq.question: Which languages does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers TypeScript with the MCP SDK and Python with FastMCP patterns.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete server automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance, examples, and review criteria that you apply to your own server.

faq.question: Can I use it for local and remote MCP servers?
faq.answer: Yes. It discusses stdio for local integrations and streamable HTTP for remote or multi-client deployments.

faq.question: How does it address security?
faq.answer: It recommends secure authentication, input validation, command injection prevention, safe errors, and DNS rebinding protection.

faq.question: What are the included evaluation scripts for?
faq.answer: They help test whether an MCP server enables Claude to answer realistic questions using the server tools.
